items.name: Ethereum
items.note: outpaced Bitcoin's rebound on the same cool-inflation tailwind

items.name: CrowdStrike
items.note: jumped after a joint US/international advisory on Russian state-sponsored infrastructure threats, plus tailwind from IBM's spending commentary

items.name: Zscaler
items.note: back above its tracked stop after today's sector-wide cybersecurity rally

items.name: ServiceNow
items.note: price now consistent across tiers; hit by the IBM-driven software pullback

outlook.summary: Not financial advice — this is a read of the public data and news above, meant to help you decide, not a recommendation. Always size positions and confirm with your own research.

strongest.reason: CrowdStrike and Zscaler both jumped 8-10% after a joint US/international advisory on Russian state-sponsored threats to critical infrastructure, and after IBM's CEO said enterprise clients are pausing other spending to prioritize security. ZS moved back clear of its tracked stop on the move.

Micron and other memory names rebounded roughly 4-5% as a cooler-than-expected June CPI print eased rate-hike fears and as some enterprise budget appears to be shifting toward hardware. TSMC also reported a 36% jump in quarterly sales and 67.9% year-over-year June revenue growth, reinforcing that AI compute demand remains intact even after last week's memory-stock selloff.

weakest.reason: IBM issued a Q2 warning (revenue growth of just 1%, EPS well below consensus) and its CEO said clients are pausing new software deals to reassess cybersecurity and hardware spending. ServiceNow, Salesforce, Adobe, and Microsoft all traded down on the day as a result.

ORCL hit a fresh 14-month low, extending its slide on concerns about high debt levels and execution risk tied to its AI data-center buildout — a separate, company-specific overhang from the broader software pullback.

outlook.nextStep: The IBM-driven divergence between cybersecurity (strength) and enterprise software (weakness) is worth a closer look since it could reshape how the SaaS cohort gets priced this earnings season; today's memory-chip rebound is better watched than acted on until it's clearer whether it's a durable re-rating or just a one-day bounce on cooler inflation data.

signals.title: Cool June CPI sharply cuts July rate-hike odds
signals.body: June CPI rose 3.5% year-over-year, below the 3.8% economists expected, and the monthly reading actually fell 0.4%. Odds of a July 29 Fed rate hike dropped to roughly 17% from over 40% earlier in the week, though traders still see close to a 60% chance of a hike by the September meeting given lingering oil-driven inflation risk from the Iran conflict.
signals.watch: Whether Warsh's upcoming testimony and the July 28-29 FOMC meeting confirm the market's lower rate-hike odds.
signals.focus: Worth a closer look — this single data point moved yields, crypto, and growth-stock sentiment all in the same direction today.

signals.title: IBM's warning splits the market: cybersecurity soars, SaaS craters
signals.body: IBM issued a Q2 earnings warning (revenue growth of just 1%, EPS of $2.27 versus a $3.02 consensus) and its CEO said enterprise clients are pausing software deals to reassess cybersecurity and hardware spending amid new threat concerns. CrowdStrike and Zscaler both jumped roughly 9-10%, while ServiceNow, Salesforce, Adobe, and Microsoft all traded lower.
signals.watch: Whether other software vendors echo IBM's commentary on delayed deals in their own upcoming earnings calls.
signals.focus: Worth a closer look — this looks like a genuine reallocation of enterprise IT budget, not just one company's guidance miss.

signals.title: Memory names rebound as TSMC posts a blowout quarter
signals.body: Micron and other memory stocks bounced back after last week's SK Hynix-driven selloff, helped by cooler CPI data and reports that some enterprise budgets are shifting toward hardware and storage ahead of expected price increases. TSMC separately reported a 36% jump in quarterly sales and 67.9% year-over-year June revenue growth, signaling AI accelerator demand remains strong even as memory pricing stays volatile.
signals.watch: Whether ASML's Q2 report (due this morning) and guidance corroborate continued strength in AI-linked capital equipment spending.
signals.focus: Lower conviction on chasing the bounce — worth letting a few more sessions confirm this is a stabilization and not just a one-day relief rally.

signals.title: Humanoid-robotics IPO adds a new supply-chain name to watch
signals.body: Agility Robotics completed its public debut via a SPAC merger, and the listing has periodically lifted humanoid-adjacent supply-chain names like Vishay Precision Group on new order news, even as lidar maker Ouster fell sharply today. Teradyne continues to be a standout in the group, up roughly 140% year-to-date on AI-chip testing demand plus its Universal Robots business.
signals.watch: Whether Agility's post-IPO trading and order backlog commentary spill over into sentiment for other humanoid-adjacent names.
signals.focus: Speculative — the sector's six-month average return is still deeply negative, and today's moves within the group are scattered rather than uniform.

signals.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum both rally on cooler inflation data
signals.body: Bitcoin recovered from an early Asian-session dip to trade back near $64,400 (+3.8%), while Ethereum outpaced it with a roughly 6% move to near $1,875, as the cooler June CPI print reduced near-term Fed rate-hike odds. Separately, US spot Bitcoin ETFs snapped an outflow streak with about $197M in net weekly inflows, led by BlackRock's IBIT, though volumes remain modest.
signals.watch: Whether ETF inflows build on this week's reversal or prove to be a one-week blip.
signals.focus: Worth checking in on — the inflow reversal is a modest positive signal, not yet a clear trend change.

signals.title: Oracle extends its slide to a 14-month low
signals.body: ORCL fell further today, extending a stretch of underperformance tied to concerns about the company's debt load and execution risk on its AI data-center buildout. This is a separate, company-specific overhang from the broader IBM-driven software selloff hitting the rest of the sector.
signals.watch: Any updated guidance from Oracle on data-center capex timelines or debt management in its next earnings update.
signals.focus: Lower conviction right now — worth waiting for a clearer signal on the debt/execution concerns before drawing conclusions.
